text: I stand within a dim corridor, the world slanted and unfamiliar. Neon purple light pulses from behind glass, casting a surreal and somewhat isolating glow. I feel at once curious and unsettled. In the second room, bold letters—R+D—loom over a futuristic display. The words on the wall speak of innovation and connection, yet there's an impersonal air, as if I'm observing a sterile promise of the future that hasn't made space for my presence. I wonder if this cold, calculated environment truly fosters human curiosity or if it merely quarantines it, boxed in by immaculate wires and distant screens.
